nation: Fond du Lac Denesuline First Nation
economic_notes: First Nation operates through Fond du Lac First Nation Development Limited Partnership (99.9%), Fond du Lac First Nation (2008) Development Corporation (100%), Waterfound Development Limited Partnership (33.33%), and Waterfound Development Corp (33.33%). GBEs manage investments including airplanes and rental buildings. Also holds portfolio investments in CanAlaska Uranium Ltd (400,000 shares), Prince Albert Development Corporation (8.33%), and PA First Nation Business Development Limited Partnership (8.25%).
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2020 but audit was signed November 2, 2023 - over 3 years late. Three entities fully consolidated (Fond du Lac Mineral Resources Inc., Fond du Lac Power Corporation, Fond du Lac Hydro Inc.). Four GBEs on modified equity basis. Fond du Lac Specific Claim Trust excluded from consolidation as controlled by Trustees. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + AHA + CMHC + ADEA + PAGC + Trust funds. ISC deferred revenue balance of $3,735,456. First Nation guaranteed FDLFNDLP demand loans of $468,414 to First Nations Bank. Current liabilities include bank indebtedness of $783,924, accounts payable $1,649,355, deferred revenue ISC $3,735,456, due to related parties $110,632, current portion LTD $1,068,536, current portion mortgages $108,170.
